activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If the weather will improve, we'll play outside.

evaluator.answer: If the weather improves, we'll play outside

activities.explanation: Después de «if» usamos presente: «the weather improves».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If you won't charge the battery, the camera will stop.

evaluator.answer: If you don't charge the battery, the camera will stop

activities.explanation: La condición negativa normal usa «don't charge», no «won't charge».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If Sam arrive before us, he'll wait in reception.

evaluator.answer: If Sam arrives before us, he'll wait in reception

activities.explanation: La tercera persona singular del presente es «arrives».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If the café is full, we will tried the bakery next door.

evaluator.answer: If the café is full, we will try the bakery next door

activities.explanation: Después de «will» usamos el infinitivo «try».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: You'll get wet if you won't take an umbrella.

evaluator.answer: You'll get wet if you don't take an umbrella

activities.explanation: Tras «if» corresponde la negativa de presente «don't take».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If the machine overheats, to switch it off immediately.

evaluator.answer: If the machine overheats, switch it off immediately

activities.explanation: El imperativo usa el verbo base sin «to»: «switch it off».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If the parcel arrives today, we might delivered it tomorrow.

evaluator.answer: If the parcel arrives today, we might deliver it tomorrow

activities.explanation: Después de «might» usamos infinitivo: «deliver».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: Unless you don't hurry, the doors will close.

evaluator.answer: Unless you hurry, the doors will close

activities.explanation: «Unless» ya aporta la negación; debe seguir «you hurry».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If Maya have time, she may come with us.

evaluator.answer: If Maya has time, she may come with us

activities.explanation: Con tercera persona singular usamos «has».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: We won't approve the payment if the details won't match.

evaluator.answer: We won't approve the payment if the details don't match

activities.explanation: La condición negativa usa «don't match».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If the tests fail, we might to postpone the launch.

evaluator.answer: If the tests fail, we might postpone the launch

activities.explanation: «Might» se combina directamente con infinitivo, sin «to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If your symptoms will continue, you should contact a doctor.

evaluator.answer: If your symptoms continue, you should contact a doctor

activities.explanation: La condición futura posible usa presente «continue».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If the room gets too warm open the window.

evaluator.answer: If the room gets too warm, open the window

activities.explanation: Cuando la cláusula con «if» aparece primero, debe separarse con una coma.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If Leo catches the earlier train he should arrive by noon.

evaluator.answer: If Leo catches the earlier train, he should arrive by noon

activities.explanation: La cláusula condicional inicial requiere una coma antes de la principal.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: You won't see the menu unless you will scroll down.

evaluator.answer: You won't see the menu unless you scroll down

activities.explanation: Después de «unless» usamos presente «scroll».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: Provided that everyone will agree, we'll publish the schedule.

evaluator.answer: Provided that everyone agrees, we'll publish the schedule

activities.explanation: «Provided that» introduce una condición y lleva presente «agrees».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: As long as everyone follow the rules, the event can continue.

evaluator.answer: As long as everyone follows the rules, the event can continue

activities.explanation: «Everyone» es singular y exige «follows».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: Even if the price will fall, I won't replace this laptop.

evaluator.answer: Even if the price falls, I won't replace this laptop

activities.explanation: Tras «even if» corresponde presente «falls».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If the client approve the design, production will start next week.

evaluator.answer: If the client approves the design, production will start next week

activities.explanation: El sujeto singular «client» requiere «approves».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If the password is too simple, the system may rejects it.

evaluator.answer: If the password is too simple, the system may reject it

activities.explanation: Después de «may» usamos el infinitivo «reject».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If the evidence remain consistent, the researchers are likely to continue.

evaluator.answer: If the evidence remains consistent, the researchers are likely to continue

activities.explanation: «Evidence» es singular incontable y se combina con «remains».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: Unless both sides will compromise, the dispute won't end.

evaluator.answer: Unless both sides compromise, the dispute won't end

activities.explanation: «Unless» debe ir seguido de presente «compromise».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If the backup works, we should can recover every file.

evaluator.answer: If the backup works, we should be able to recover every file

activities.explanation: No combinamos dos modales; tras «should» usamos «be able to».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If the chairperson object, we'll hold another vote.

evaluator.answer: If the chairperson objects, we'll hold another vote

activities.explanation: La tercera persona singular del presente es «objects».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the future conditional error.
activities.prompt: If you'll wait here, the assistant will to check the diary.

evaluator.answer: If you'll wait here, the assistant will check the diary

activities.explanation: El «will» cortés tras «if» es válido; el error está en «will to check», que debe ser «will check».
